articles.title: A vitamin A discovery is changing what scientists know about vision
articles.summary: A surprising discovery is reshaping scientists' understanding of how humans develop sharp central vision before birth. Instead of blue cone cells migrating away from the retina's center, the study found they transform into red and green cones under the influence of vitamin A-related signals and thyroid hormones.

articles.title: Hawaii's famous “happy-face” spider has a surprising relative
articles.summary: A newly discovered Happy-Face spider in the Himalayas closely resembles Hawaii's iconic species but evolved independently, according to DNA evidence. Its mysterious smile-like markings, many color forms, and unexpected link to ginger plants have scientists eager to learn how the two distant species are connected.

articles.title: This alien planet never has sunrise or sunset. It may support life
articles.summary: A planet with one side permanently roasting and the other frozen in endless darkness might still have a chance of supporting life. articles.title: Physicists created a tiny universe where time emerged without a clock
articles.summary: What if time doesn't actually exist until something changes?
